text.*:
Ranked is a competitive mode unlocked from the Trophy Road at 1000 Trophies. It replaced Power League in the February 2024 update. In Ranked, Brawlers below Power Level 9 cannot be played; in Mythic Ranks and above, Brawlers below Power Level 11 cannot be played. Players can progress to higher Ranks, or drop to lower Ranks, based on the outcome of each Ranked match.

Every month, a season ends and the next one begins, and every player's Rank is reset when a new season begins. Trophies cannot be gained or lost in this mode.

Gameplay[]

In a Ranked match, players will play a random 3v3 mode on a random map. At Mythic I and above, both teams have a Team Captain, which is either decided by the player with the highest Elo, or by the host of the game room (if playing in teams). Team Captains get the last pick in the drafting phase.

From Bronze I to Gold III, like Mega Pig matches, each player on both teams blindly picks a Brawler, and equips any Star Powers, Gadgets, and Gears without any extra delay prior to starting the match. If a player doesn't pick a Brawler within the 20 second time limit, the match is cancelled and all players are disconnected to the lobby.

From Diamond I, a player needs at least 9 Power Level 9 Brawlers that can be played, where the temporarily maxed out Brawlers count towards the requirement. Players start by banning Brawlers before starting a match, preventing anyone on either team from selecting them once 6 bans have been picked. During the ban phase, players can only see the bans of their own team, and players from the same team cannot ban the same brawler more than once (but it is possible that both teams ban the same Brawler). If the 20 second timer runs out, a random Brawler will be banned if no selection was attempted. Then players pick as normal and the match starts.

Starting from Mythic I, like Power League, Ranked matches are formatted as a best-of-3 tournament where the first team to win 2 matches is the winner. It is required to have at least 12 Power Level 11 Brawlers that can be played. A coin flip occurs once the matchmaking is complete that determines which team gets the first pick. Bans occur simultaneously before Brawlers are selected in a snake draft format, and players from both teams choose the Brawlers one by one, with each player allotted 20 seconds to choose their Brawler. Players who pre-picked are able to change their load-out of Gadgets, Star Powers, and Gears regardless of their turn in drafting. Additionally, after all Brawlers are picked, all players have 15 seconds to choose Star Powers, Gadgets, and Gears, if applicable. They also have the option to swap Brawlers with a teammate, provided both players have Power Level 11 on the swapped Brawlers. Players cannot pick Brawlers that have already been banned or picked by another player.

Ranks[]

Players are matched based on their current Elo. The higher the Elo of your opponents, the more Elo you earn. Players can queue solo, in a team of 2, or in a team of 3, except in Masters I and above where players can only queue solo.

Once a player reaches a new Rank, they can get demoted to a lower Rank. For Ranks up to Gold, a player cannot demote below the full Rank for the duration of the season. For example, once players get to Gold I, they cannot drop to Silver III. Starting from Diamond, players can demote below the full Rank if their Elo is excactly at the threshold. For example, once players get to Mythic I, they cannot drop to Diamond III if they have more than 4500 points, unless a game is lost at 4500 points. However, it is still possible to be demoted within a Rank. For example, losing matches in Gold II can demote the player back to Gold I, and there is no floor protection in this case.

The amount of Elo gained or lost after a match depends on your current Rank and the outcome of the match, as well as the Elo of your opponents. When playing solo, the amount of Elo earned depends on your team's average Elo in comparison to the enemy team's average Elo. When playing in a team, the amount of Elo earned depends on your own Elo in comparison to the enemy team's average Elo. If your own or your team's Elo is higher than the enemy team's, you will get less Elo for a win and lose more for a loss. If your own or your team's Elo is lower than the enemy team's, you will get more Elo for a win and lose less for a loss. If you have an active Ranked boost, you will gain an additional 10% Elo on victory, always being rounded down. There's a minimum amount of Elo you can gain or lose depending on your Rank.

Seasons[]

Ranked seasons start on the third Thursday of every month. At the end of a season, if your Rank is above Silver I, your final Rank decreases by 6 Ranks, or until Silver I, whichever results in less Ranks being lost. However, you receive a 10% boost until you reach the highest Rank you reached the previous season or the Legendary I Rank, whichever comes first. Players are rewarded with 400 Pro Pass XP upon reaching new Ranks every season. Ranked Starr Drops contain one reward, such as Bling, Pro Pass XP, and cosmetics from previous Power League and Ranked seasons. Exclusive Battle Cards are available when promoting to a new full Rank for the current season. Reaching the same Rank across multiple seasons also adds a star to the Battle Card. Each Battle Card maxes out after you reach that Rank 7 times.

Trial Brawlers[]

Each season, 3 maxed Brawlers are available for everyone to use in Ranked, even if players haven't unlocked them yet. Players that pick maxed Brawlers can play them at Power Level 11 with all Gadgets, Star Powers, Gears, and Hypercharge unlocked, like in Friendly Battles. These maxed Brawlers count towards the Brawler requirements for each Rank (e.g. having to have more than 12 Power Level 11 Brawlers in Mythic and above). Since April 16th 2026, any maxed Brawlers with Buffies will also have them during the trial period.

Modifiers[]

This section describes content that has been replaced or removed from the game. This information is kept here for historical purposes.

Each season featured 4 Modifiers, with the Classic Modifier being permanent, and the other 3 rotating every season. In the February 2025 update, Modifiers were removed from Ranked.

Reporting System[]

The Reporting System is a feature that allows the player to report other players from their own and the enemy team at the end of a Ranked match for intentionally disrupting the playing experience for everyone involved. There are 3 types of report that can be made, which are Bad Random, Grief Play, and Cheating.

Reputation Meter[]

Each player has an individual Reputation Meter. The Reputation will drop when they get reported for Grief Play or Cheating, leaving a Ranked match too early, or disconnecting during the Brawler ban/selection screen. Reporting for Bad Random does nothing. When their Reputation has dropped, they will get a Yellow Card, warning them that more penalties will result in suspension from Ranked matches, and when their Reputation has dropped to zero, they will get banned from playing Ranked for 30 minutes. Players can only recover Reputation for each Ranked game where they didn't get disconnected or reported.

History[]

  • On 27/02/24, Ranked was added to the game and replaced Power League.
  • On 04/03/24, server changes were applied to improve matchmaking.
  • On 06/03/24, fixed an issue with players teaming up in Duos.
  • On 25/03/24, Solo players facing teams now receive Elo as if they are playing against similarly ranked players. Wins now always give a minimum amount of Elo, and the maximum amount of Elo gained for losses has been reduced. Made general changes to Elo distribution to reduce inflation.
  • On 23/04/24, matchmaking will now favor teams playing against other teams. Elo gain for solo players will now consider the team average Elo against the opposing team's. Adjusted the Elo gain in Legendary II up to Masters. Rank Boost will now give a higher boost depending on how high you reached in the previous Ranked season. Players in the Bronze to Gold Ranks now have a higher chance of getting Modifiers in their matches. Probability of getting Modifiers for players in Masters was reduced.
  • On 25/04/24, fixed Ranked Boost stopping one Rank lower than it should. Ranked Boost was rebalanced: Bronze (no boost), Silver (10%), Gold (20%), Diamond (30%), Mythic (50%), Legendary (75%), Masters (100%).
  • On 03/05/24, Second Wind Modifier now has a 1% chance of being selected. Fixed the Ranked Star not appearing when the player gets to the same Rank twice or more.
  • On 12/09/24, fixed a bug making matchmaking longer than supposed.
  • On 04/10/24, fixed the issue that caused the Ranked Boost, Ranked Battle Cards and Previous Ranked season leaderboards to be missing. Fixed an issue where the Malicious Mandy skin was unavailable in Ranked Starr Drops. The Malicious Mandy skin could be obtained for free by reaching Legendary I during Ranked Season 26.
  • On 29/10/24, lowered Elo gain and minimum Elo gain for Legendary III and Masters (from +25 to +10), and increased Elo loss and minimum Elo loss for Masters.
  • On 31/10/24, fixed unusual behavior with Win Streak while playing Ranked.
  • On 25/02/25, Ranked was reworked, and the Pro Pass was added. Ranked seasons now start on the third Thursday of every month. Winning Ranked matches and ranking up now awards Pro Pass XP. Added Masters II and III, and the Pro Rank. Bronze and Silver now takes 250 Elo, Legendary takes 750 Elo, and Masters takes 1000 Elo to advance each Rank. Adjusted floor protection for Mythic and up. The Elo distribution was changed. Reduced Ranked Boost to 10%. Teaming up in higher Ranks is more restrictive. Matchmaking no longer increases Elo search range outside of your Rank restriction. Rank reset now resets players by 6 Ranks above Diamond I. Mythic and above now requires Power 11 Brawlers. Modifiers were removed. Each season, 3 maxed Brawlers are available for everyone in Ranked. Added Pro Pass XP in Ranked Starr Drops. A new set of Ranked Battle Cards were added.
  • On 03/03/25, Bounty was added back to the rotation and the number of maps for each mode was increased from 3 to 4, increasing the total number of maps from 15 to 24.
  • On 04/03/25, fixed various visual issues related to Ranked.
  • On 12/03/25, fixed various visual issues related to Ranked.
  • On 17/04/25, Hot Zone was replaced by Heist in the rotation. Brawl Hockey was replaced by Cleaning Duty.
  • On 30/04/25, fixed a bug preventing Gadget selection for free maxed out Brawlers if the player already owned one.
  • On 15/05/25, Cleaning Duty was replaced by Hot Zone in the rotation.
  • On 19/06/25, Ranked seasons will now have one of the modes selected as a featured mode for that season, which will put additional maps of that mode in the map rotation.
  • On 24/06/25, Elo loss from Legendary III and above was reduced to mostly flat Elo. The time limit to meet same opponents was reduced from 12 hours to 1 hour.
  • On 17/07/25, Masters and above can only queue solo.
  • On 23/07/25, fixed a bug with the Ranked leaderboard.
  • On 13/08/25, fixed incorrect end-of-battle screen stats for matches below Mythic. Fixed a bug where Ranked Starr Drops were not working properly and were only rewarding Bling and Pro Pass XP.
  • On 17/09/25, fixed a bug where players were stuck in matchmaking.
  • On 25/02/26, fixed an edge case that could cause a crash in the loadout screen.
  • On 22/04/26, the limit of 10 reports per day was removed.
